327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]

628	    def process_name_for_reasoning(self, name):
629	        if not name:
630	            raise ValueError("name must be provided")
631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"
632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"

16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
18	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
19	    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs
196

124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127

129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197

76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs
196

123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197

199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

5	    # test whether an object is a legacy structural tag
6	    # see `StructuralTagResponseFormat` at `sglang.srt.entrypoints.openai.protocol`
7	    if obj.get("structures", None) is not None:

12	        return False

2109	        has_been_filtered = v1_spec_info_filtered and not self.is_spec_v2
2110	
2111	        if self.spec_info:
2112	            self.spec_info.filter_batch(
2113	                new_indices=keep_indices_device,
2114	                has_been_filtered=has_been_filtered,
2115	            )
2116	
2117	    def merge_batch(self, other: "ScheduleBatch"):
2118	        # NOTE: in spec v2 mode, we do not need wait verify here because
2119	        # 1) current batch is always prefill, whose seq_lens is not a future
2120	        # 2) other batch is always decode, which is finished in previous step
2121	
2122	        # Penalizer orchestrator must be merged before Batch.reqs is merged. This is because
2123	        # orchestrator.merge() depends on Batch.reqs during preparation of each penalizers, so it
2124	        # needs to be called with pre-merged Batch.reqs.
2125	        self.sampling_info.merge_batch(other.sampling_info)
2126	
2127	        # Encoder-decoder infos
2128	        if self.model_config.is_encoder_decoder:
2129	            self.encoder_lens = torch.cat([self.encoder_lens, other.encoder_lens])
2130	            self.encoder_lens_cpu.extend(other.encoder_lens_cpu)
2131	        self.req_pool_indices = torch.cat(
2132	            [self.req_pool_indices, other.req_pool_indices]
2133	        )
2134	        self.seq_lens = torch.cat([self.seq_lens, other.seq_lens])
2135	        self.seq_lens_cpu = torch.cat([self.seq_lens_cpu, other.seq_lens_cpu])
2136	        self.orig_seq_lens = torch.cat([self.orig_seq_lens, other.orig_seq_lens])
2137	        self.out_cache_loc = None
2138	        self.seq_lens_sum += other.seq_lens_sum
2139	        if self.output_ids is not None:
2140	            self.output_ids = torch.cat([self.output_ids, other.output_ids])
2141	        self.mamba_track_indices = None
2142	        self.mamba_track_mask = None
2143	        self.mamba_track_seqlens = None
2144	        if self.return_logprob and other.return_logprob:
2145	            self.top_logprobs_nums.extend(other.top_logprobs_nums)
2146	            self.token_ids_logprobs.extend(other.token_ids_logprobs)
2147	        elif self.return_logprob:
2148	            self.top_logprobs_nums.extend([0] * len(other.reqs))
2149	            self.token_ids_logprobs.extend([None] * len(other.reqs))
2150	        elif other.return_logprob:
2151	            self.top_logprobs_nums = [0] * len(self.reqs) + other.top_logprobs_nums
2152	            self.token_ids_logprobs = [None] * len(self.reqs) + other.token_ids_logprobs
2153	        self.reqs.extend(other.reqs)
2154	        if self.multimodal_inputs is not None:
2155	            self.multimodal_inputs.extend(other.multimodal_inputs)
2156	
2157	        self.return_logprob |= other.return_logprob
2158	        self.has_stream |= other.has_stream
2159	        self.has_grammar |= other.has_grammar
2160	        self.return_hidden_states |= other.return_hidden_states
2161	
2162	        if self.spec_info:
2163	            self.spec_info.merge_batch(other.spec_info)
2164	
2165	    def get_model_worker_batch(
2166	        self, seq_lens_cpu_cache: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
2167	    ) -> ModelWorkerBatch:
2168	        if self.forward_mode.is_decode_or_idle():
2169	            extend_seq_lens = extend_prefix_lens = extend_logprob_start_lens = None
2170	        else:
2171	            extend_seq_lens = self.extend_lens
2172	            extend_prefix_lens = self.prefix_lens
2173	            extend_logprob_start_lens = self.extend_logprob_start_lens
2174	
2175	        if self.sampling_info:
2176	            if self.has_grammar:
2177	                self.sampling_info.grammars = [req.grammar for req in self.reqs]
2178	            else:
2179	                self.sampling_info.grammars = None
2180	
2181	        seq_lens_cpu = (
2182	            seq_lens_cpu_cache if seq_lens_cpu_cache is not None else self.seq_lens_cpu
2183	        )
2184	
2185	        return ModelWorkerBatch(
2186	            forward_mode=self.forward_mode,
2187	            input_ids=self.input_ids,
2188	            req_pool_indices=self.req_pool_indices,
2189	            seq_lens=self.seq_lens,
2190	            orig_seq_lens=self.orig_seq_lens,
2191	            out_cache_loc=self.out_cache_loc,
2192	            seq_lens_cpu=seq_lens_cpu,
2193	            seq_lens_sum=self.seq_lens_sum,
2194	            return_logprob=self.return_logprob,
2195	            top_logprobs_nums=self.top_logprobs_nums,
2196	            token_ids_logprobs=self.token_ids_logprobs,
2197	            global_num_tokens=self.global_num_tokens,
2198	            global_num_tokens_for_logprob=self.global_num_tokens_for_logprob,
2199	            is_extend_in_batch=self.is_extend_in_batch,

14	"""Constrained decoding with outlines backend."""
15	
16	import json
17	import logging
18	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
19	
20	import interegular
21	import torch
22	from outlines.fsm.guide import RegexGuide
23	from outlines.models.transformers import TransformerTokenizer
24	from pydantic import BaseModel
25	
26	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
27	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
28	    BaseGrammarBackend,
29	    BaseGrammarObject,
30	)
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_jump_forward import OutlinesJumpForwardMap
32	
33	try:
34	    from outlines.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
35	except ImportError:
36	    from outlines_core.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
37	
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state
112	
113	
114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)
191

53	class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token

8	@dataclass
9	class ChoicesDecision:
10	    decision: str
11	    meta_info: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
12	
13	
14	class ChoicesSamplingMethod(ABC):
15	
16	    @property
17	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
18	        return False
19	
20	    @abstractmethod
21	    def __call__(
22	        self,
23	        *,
24	        choices: List[str],
25	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
26	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
27	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
28	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
29	    ) -> ChoicesDecision: ...
30	
31	
32	class TokenLengthNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
33	
34	    def __call__(
35	        self,
36	        *,
37	        choices: List[str],
38	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
39	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
40	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
41	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
42	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
43	        """Select the option with the highest token length normalized prompt logprob."""
44	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_prompt_logprobs)]
45	        meta_info = {
46	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
47	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
48	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
49	        }
50	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
51	
52	
53	token_length_normalized = TokenLengthNormalized()

55	
56	class GreedyTokenSelection(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
57	
58	    def __call__(
59	        self,
60	        *,
61	        choices: List[str],
62	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
63	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
64	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
65	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
66	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
67	        """Select the option based on greedy logprob selection. For overlapping options
68	        where one option is a subset of a longer option, extend the shorter option using
69	        its average logprob for comparison against the longer option."""
70	
71	        num_options = len(choices)
72	        max_tokens = max(len(option) for option in input_token_logprobs)
73	        logprob_matrix = self._build_logprob_matrix(
74	            input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options
75	        )
76	        remaining = self._greedy_selection(logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens)
77	
78	        best_choice = choices[remaining[0]]
79	        meta_info = {
80	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
81	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
82	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
83	            "greedy_logprob_matrix": logprob_matrix.tolist(),
84	        }
85	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
86	
87	    def _build_logprob_matrix(self, input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options):
88	        logprob_matrix = np.zeros((num_options, max_tokens))
89	        for i, option in enumerate(input_token_logprobs):
90	            actual_logprobs = [token[0] for token in option]
91	            avg_logprob = np.mean(actual_logprobs)
92	            logprob_matrix[i, : len(option)] = actual_logprobs
93	            if len(option) < max_tokens:
94	                logprob_matrix[i, len(option) :] = avg_logprob
95	        return logprob_matrix
96	
97	    def _greedy_selection(self, logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens):
98	        remaining = np.arange(num_options)
99	        for j in range(max_tokens):
100	            max_logprob = np.max(logprob_matrix[remaining, j])
101	            remaining = remaining[logprob_matrix[remaining, j] == max_logprob]
102	            if len(remaining) == 1:
103	                break
104	        return remaining
105	
106	
107	greedy_token_selection = GreedyTokenSelection()
108	
109	
110	class UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
111	
112	    @property
113	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
114	        return True
115	
116	    def __call__(
117	        self,
118	        *,
119	        choices: List[str],
120	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
121	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
122	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
123	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
124	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
125	        """Select the option with the highest average token logprob once normalized by
126	        the unconditional token logprobs.
127	
128	        The first unconditional token logprob is assumed to be None. If so, it is
129	        replaced with 0 for the purposes of normalization."""
130	
131	        if unconditional_token_logprobs is None:
132	            raise ValueError(
133	                "Unconditional token logprobs are required for this method."
134	            )
135	
136	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = self._normalize_logprobs(
137	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
138	        )
139	
140	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs)]
141	        meta_info = {
142	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
143	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
144	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
145	            "unconditional_token_logprobs": unconditional_token_logprobs,
146	            "normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs": normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs,
147	        }
148	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
149	
150	    def _normalize_logprobs(self, input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs):
151	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = []
152	        for inputs, unconditionals in zip(
153	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
154	        ):
155	            inputs_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in inputs])
156	            unconditionals_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in unconditionals])
157	            unconditionals_logprobs[0] = unconditionals_logprobs[0] or 0
158	            normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs.append(
159	                float(np.mean(inputs_logprobs - unconditionals_logprobs))
160	            )
161	        return normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs
162	
163	
164	unconditional_likelihood_normalized = UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized()

109	class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	
111	    def __init__(
112	        self,
113	        tokenizer,
114	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
115	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
116	        n_vocab: Optional[int] = None,
117	    ):
118	        super().__init__()
119	
120	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
121	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
122	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
123	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = from_tokenizer(self.tokenizer, n_vocab)
124	
125	    def _from_serialized(self, serialized_grammar) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
126	        try:
127	            return GuidanceGrammar(
128	                llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
129	                serialized_grammar=serialized_grammar,
130	            )
131	        except Exception as e:
132	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid grammar: {serialized_grammar=}, {e=}")
133	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
134	
135	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
136	        try:
137	            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
138	                key_string,
139	                defaults={
140	                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
141	                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
142	                },
143	            )
144	        except Exception as e:
145	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
146	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
147	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
148	
149	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
150	        serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
151	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
152	
153	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
154	        try:
155	            serialized_grammar = grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)
156	            return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
157	        except ValueError as e:
158	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
159	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
160	
161	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
162	        try:
163	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
164	            assert is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag)
165	            tags = [
166	                StructTag(
167	                    begin=structure["begin"],
168	                    grammar=structure["schema"],
169	                    end=structure["end"],
170	                    trigger=structural_tag["triggers"][0],  # TODO?
171	                )
172	                for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
173	            ]
174	            g = StructTag.to_grammar(tags)
175	            return self._from_serialized(g)
176	        except Exception as e:
177	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
178	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the constraint architecture. Let me create a detailed report:

## Summary of SGLang Output Constraints Implementation

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a comprehensive report on how output constraints are implemented and registered:

### 1. **Constraint Types Supported**

SGLang supports multiple constraint types defined in the sampling parameters:
- **JSON Schema** (`json_schema`) - Constrains output to match a JSON schema
- **Regex** (`regex`) - Constrains output to match a regular expression pattern
- **EBNF** (`ebnf`) - Constrains output to match Extended Backus-Naur Form grammar
- **Structural Tag** (`structural_tag`) - For structured outputs
- **Stop Regex** (`stop_regex`) - Regex-based stop tokens

**Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` (Lines 40-87, 155-161)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` (Lines 36-40)

### 2. **Constraint Registry Pattern**

SGLang uses a **factory pattern** with a backend registry for constraint implementation:

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`

- **Line 199:** `GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}` - Global registry dictionary
- **Line 202-203:** `register_grammar_backend(name, init_func)` - Registration function
- **Lines 206-274:** `create_grammar_backend()` - Factory function that creates backend instances

**Registered Backends:**
1. **Outlines Backend** - Uses the `outlines` library with interegular for regex/JSON
2. **XGrammar Backend** - Uses `xgrammar` library with token bitmasks
3. **LLGuidance Backend** - Uses `llguidance` library
4. **ReasonerGrammar Wrapper** - Wraps other backends for reasoning support

### 3. **Core Constraint Classes and Interfaces**

**Base Class:** `BaseGrammarObject`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (Lines 42-118)

**Key Methods:**
- `accept_token(token: int)` - Accept a token in the grammar (Line 52)
- `allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)` - Create vocab mask (Line 64)
- `fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)` - Fill allowed tokens (Line 69)
- `apply_vocab_mask(logits, vocab_mask)` - Apply mask to logits (Line 77)
- `try_jump_forward(tokenizer)` - Jump forward in grammar (Line 91)
- `copy()` - Create a copy for batch processing (Line 80)

**Implementation Classes:**
1. **OutlinesGrammar** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` (Lines 42-112)
2. **XGrammarGrammar** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` (Lines 53-150)

### 4. **Constraint Dispatch System**

**Base Backend Methods (Lines 145-175 in base_grammar_backend.py):**
```python
dispatch_json(key_string: str) - Handle JSON schema constraints
dispatch_regex(key_string: str) - Handle regex constraints  
dispatch_ebnf(key_string: str) - Handle EBNF constraints
dispatch_structural_tag(key_string: str) - Handle structural tags
_init_value_dispatch(key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool) - Main dispatcher
```

The dispatcher uses a key tuple format: `(constraint_type: str, constraint_value: str)`
- `("json", json_schema_string)`
- `("regex", regex_string)`
- `("ebnf", ebnf_string)`
- `("structural_tag", tag_string)`

### 5. **Grammar Manager - Main Constraint Orchestrator**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

**Class:** `GrammarManager` (Line 24)

**Key Methods:**
- `process_req_with_grammar(req: Req) -> bool` (Line 67) - Check if request needs grammar and cache/compile it
- `get_ready_grammar_requests() -> List[Req]` (Line 107) - Poll for ready grammars and handle timeouts
- `abort_requests(recv_req: AbortReq)` (Line 59) - Abort grammar compilation

**Integration Point:** Instantiated in Scheduler at line 413 of `scheduler.py`

### 6. **Constraint Compilation and Caching**

**Cache System:**
- **Lines 124-127** in base_grammar_backend.py: `CacheEntry` dataclass with value and Event
- **Line 132:** `self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry]`
- **Lines 181-190:** `get_cached_or_future_value()` - Returns either cached value or Future

**Threading Model:**
- Uses `ThreadPoolExecutor` (Line 131) for asynchronous grammar compilation
- Futures are used to track compilation progress
- Grammar manager polls futures with timeout mechanism

### 7. **Constraint Parsing from User Input**

**API Layer:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/lang/api.py`

**Function:** `gen()` (Lines 75-139)
- Accepts `regex`, `json_schema` parameters
- Validates regex with `re.compile()` (Line 113)
- Creates `SglGen` IR node with constraints

**Protocol Layer:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

**Classes:**
- `CompletionRequest` (Lines 268-270) - Accepts regex, ebnf, json_schema
- `ChatCompletionRequest` (Lines 580-584) - Same constraint fields
- `set_json_schema()` validator (Lines 669-756) - Converts response_format to json_schema

**Validation:** SamplingParams.verify() (Lines 155-161 in sampling_params.py)
- Ensures only ONE constraint is set at a time

### 8. **Constraint Application in Decoding**

**Request Flow:**
1. **Tokenizer Manager** - Checks if request has grammar (tokenizer_manager.py, Line 1937)
2. **Scheduler** - Creates grammar compilation tasks (scheduler.py, Line 1641)
3. **Grammar Manager** - Manages async compilation queue
4. **Schedule Batch** - Attaches grammar objects to batch (schedule_batch.py, Lines 2176-2179)
5. **Sampler/Logits Processor** - Applies vocab mask during token sampling

**Files:**
- Tokenizer manager: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py` (Lines 1937-1943)
- Scheduler: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/managers/scheduler.py` (Lines 413, 1641, 1981-1982)
- Schedule Batch: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/managers/schedule_batch.py` (Lines 749-751, 2103, 2177)

### 9. **Test Coverage**

Test files for constraints:
- **JSON Schema:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py`
- **Regex Tests:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py`
- **JSON Tests:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py`
- **EBNF Tests:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py`

### 10. **Key Helper Functions**

**Schema Conversion:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py`
  - `get_json_schema_constraint()` (Line 207) - Converts tool choices to JSON schema
  - `_get_tool_schema_defs()` (Line 60) - Consolidates $defs from tools
  - `infer_type_from_json_schema()` (Line 104) - Type inference from schemas

**Regex Processing:**
- `get_max_seq_length()` (Line 202 in sampling_params.py) - Calculates buffer size for regex matching
- Parses regex using `sre_parse` module

This architecture provides a flexible, extensible system for adding new constraint types through the registry pattern, with clear separation between constraint definition, compilation, and application during token generation.

| `outlines_backend.py` | Outlines FSM backend | `OutlinesGrammar`, `OutlinesGrammarBackend` |
| `xgrammar_backend.py` | XGrammar compiled backend | `XGrammarGrammar`, `XGrammarGrammarBackend` |
| `llguidance_backend.py` | LLGuidance token backend | `GuidanceGrammar`, `GuidanceBackend` |
| `sampling_batch_info.py` | Batched sampling state | `SamplingBatchInfo.update_regex_vocab_mask()`, `apply_logits_bias()` |

- Triggers request abort with error message

**Token Validation Failures:**
- Caught in `scheduler_output_processor_mixin.py` 
- Request aborted with ValueError details
- Logged for debugging

**No external libraries used:** SGLang does NOT use `jsonpath`, `jsonpath_ng`, or `jsonpath_rw`. All JSON schema handling is done via grammar compilation (regex extraction) rather than runtime path validation.

**Allocation** (Backend-specific):
- **XGrammar**: Uses `allocate_token_bitmask()` for bitmask format
- **Outlines**: Uses `torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool)`
- **LLGuidance**: Caches bitmask allocation

**Application** (Backend-specific):
- **XGrammar**: `apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton()` or CUDA kernel
- **Outlines**: `logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))`
- **LLGuidance**: `apply_token_bitmask_inplace()`

### 8. **Key Files Summary**

| File Path | Purpose | Key Classes |
|-----------|---------|------------|
| `sampling_params.py` | Parameter definition & regex parsing | `SamplingParams`, `get_max_seq_length()` |
| `grammar_manager.py` | Grammar compilation coordination | `GrammarManager` |
| `base_grammar_backend.py` | Abstract backend interface | `BaseGrammarBackend`, `BaseGrammarObject` |
| `outlines_backend.py` | Outlines FSM backend | `OutlinesGrammar`, `OutlinesGrammarBackend` |
| `xgrammar_backend.py` | XGrammar compiled backend | `XGrammarGrammar`, `XGrammarGrammarBackend` |
| `llguidance_backend.py` | LLGuidance token backend | `GuidanceGrammar`, `GuidanceBackend` |
| `sampling_batch_info.py` | Batched sampling state | `SamplingBatchInfo.update_regex_vocab_mask()`, `apply_logits_bias()` |
| `model_runner.py` | Forward pass integration | `_preprocess_logits()`, `sample()` |
| `schedule_batch.py` | Batch management | Grammar assignment to SamplingBatchInfo |
| `scheduler_output_processor_mixin.py` | Result processing | Token acceptance validation |

### 9. **Error Handling**

**Invalid Grammar Handling:**
- Cached as `INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ` sentinel object
- Triggers request abort with error message
- Timeout handling: `SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS` env var

**Token Validation Failures:**
- Caught in `scheduler_output_processor_mixin.py` 
- Request aborted with ValueError details
- Logged for debugging

**No external libraries used:** SGLang does NOT use `jsonpath`, `jsonpath_ng`, or `jsonpath_rw`. All JSON schema handling is done via grammar compilation (regex extraction) rather than runtime path validation.

```python
def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    if sampling_params.dtype is None:
        return
    
    dtype_regex = None
    if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_INT                          # r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
        sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT                        # r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
        sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_STR                          # r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL                         # r"(True|False)"
    
    if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
        warnings.warn("Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used.")
    
    sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
```

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
            key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
        elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
            key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
        
        # Get cached or compile grammar (async)
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
            key, req.require_reasoning
        )
        req.grammar = value
        
        if not cache_hit:
            req.grammar_key = key
            add_to_grammar_queue = True  # Queue for async compilation
```

#### **`OutlinesGrammarBackend` Class (Lines 114-178)**
```python
class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
        """Converts JSON schema to regex for constrained generation"""
        try:
            regex = build_regex_from_object(
                key_string,
                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
            )
        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._compile_regex(regex)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
        """Compiles regex pattern to FSM for token masking"""
        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
    
    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
        """Creates RegexGuide from regex pattern"""
        guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map=None)
```

#### **`OutlinesGrammar` Class (Lines 42-112)**
Runtime grammar object that manages state during generation:
```python
class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
    def accept_token(self, token: int):
        """Update FSM state after token is generated"""
        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        """Set allowed tokens based on current FSM state"""
        tokens = torch.tensor(
            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens
        )
        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
        vocab_mask.fill_(1)  # Block all tokens
        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, 0)  # Allow next valid tokens
```

## Summary: Constraint Flow Path

```
User Code
    ↓
sgl.gen(regex="...", dtype=int, json_schema="{...}", choices=["A","B"])
    ↓
/python/sglang/lang/api.py:gen()
    - Validates regex syntax
    - Returns SglGen (with SglSelect if choices provided)
    ↓
/python/sglang/lang/ir.py:SglGen (or SglSelect)
    - Wraps params in SglSamplingParams
    - dtype, regex, json_schema stored
    ↓
/python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py:_execute_gen()
    - Resolves sampling params with defaults
    - Calls backend.generate(sampling_params)
    ↓
/python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py:generate()
    - Converts dtype → regex if needed
    - Calls sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs()
    - Sends HTTP POST to /generate with params
    ↓
/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py:process_req_with_grammar()
    - Detects json_schema/regex/ebnf constraints
    - Routes to appropriate grammar backend
    - Queues for async compilation if not cached
    ↓
/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py or xgrammar_backend.py
    - dispatch_json() → build_regex_from_object()
    - dispatch_regex() → RegexGuide / GrammarCompiler
    - Creates FSM for token-level masking
    ↓
Generation Loop:
    - accept_token() updates FSM state
    - fill_vocab_mask() sets allowed tokens
    - apply_vocab_mask() -∞ to disallowed tokens in logits
```

```python
def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    """Merge default sampling params with generation-specific overrides"""

    # Lines 789-810: Iterates through all params including:
```

```python
def _execute_select(self, expr: SglSelect):
    choices_decision = self.backend.select(
        self, expr.choices, expr.temperature, expr.choices_method
    )
    if expr.name is not None:
        self.variables[expr.name] = choices_decision.decision
        self.meta_info[expr.name] = choices_decision.meta_info
    self.text_ += choices_decision.decision
```

### 6. **Backend Interface for Constraints**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`

#### **`_handle_dtype_to_regex()` Method (Lines 127-157)**
Converts dtype constraints to regex:
```python
def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    if sampling_params.dtype is None:
        return
    
    dtype_regex = None
    if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_INT                          # r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
        sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT                        # r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
        sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_STR                          # r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL                         # r"(True|False)"
    
    if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
        warnings.warn("Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used.")
    
    sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
```

```python
def generate(self, s: StreamExecutor, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
    
    data = {
        "text": s.text_,
        "sampling_params": {
            **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),  # Includes regex, json_schema
        },
    }
    
    res = http_request(self.base_url + "/generate", json=data)
```

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
            key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
        elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
            key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
        
        # Get cached or compile grammar (async)
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
            key, req.require_reasoning
        )
        req.grammar = value
        
        if not cache_hit:
            req.grammar_key = key
            add_to_grammar_queue = True  # Queue for async compilation
```

```python
def _init_value_dispatch(self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool):
    key_type, key_string = key
    if key_type == "json":
        grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
    elif key_type == "regex":
        grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
    elif key_type == "ebnf":
        grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
    elif key_type == "structural_tag":
        grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
    # ... others ...
    return grammar
```

### 9. **Outlines Backend Implementation**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472448691-3o7qa6/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`

#### **`OutlinesGrammarBackend` Class (Lines 114-178)**
```python
class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
        """Converts JSON schema to regex for constrained generation"""
        try:
            regex = build_regex_from_object(
                key_string,
                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
            )
        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._compile_regex(regex)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
        """Compiles regex pattern to FSM for token masking"""
        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
    
    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
        """Creates RegexGuide from regex pattern"""
        guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map=None)
```

#### **`OutlinesGrammar` Class (Lines 42-112)**
Runtime grammar object that manages state during generation:
```python
class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
    def accept_token(self, token: int):
        """Update FSM state after token is generated"""
        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        """Set allowed tokens based on current FSM state"""
        tokens = torch.tensor(
            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens
        )
        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
        vocab_mask.fill_(1)  # Block all tokens
        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, 0)  # Allow next valid tokens
```

```python
def test_regex():
    regex = r"((25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|[01]?\d\d?).){3}(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|[01]?\d\d?)"
    
    @sgl.function

        s += "A: " + sgl.gen("answer", temperature=0, regex=regex)
```

## Summary: Constraint Flow Path

```
User Code
    ↓
sgl.gen(regex="...", dtype=int, json_schema="{...}", choices=["A","B"])
    ↓
/python/sglang/lang/api.py:gen()
    - Validates regex syntax
    - Returns SglGen (with SglSelect if choices provided)
    ↓
/python/sglang/lang/ir.py:SglGen (or SglSelect)
    - Wraps params in SglSamplingParams
    - dtype, regex, json_schema stored
    ↓
/python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py:_execute_gen()
    - Resolves sampling params with defaults
    - Calls backend.generate(sampling_params)
    ↓
/python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py:generate()
    - Converts dtype → regex if needed
    - Calls sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs()
    - Sends HTTP POST to /generate with params
    ↓
/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py:process_req_with_grammar()
    - Detects json_schema/regex/ebnf constraints
    - Routes to appropriate grammar backend
    - Queues for async compilation if not cached
    ↓
/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py or xgrammar_backend.py
    - dispatch_json() → build_regex_from_object()
    - dispatch_regex() → RegexGuide / GrammarCompiler
    - Creates FSM for token-level masking
    ↓
Generation Loop:
    - accept_token() updates FSM state
    - fill_vocab_mask() sets allowed tokens
    - apply_vocab_mask() -∞ to disallowed tokens in logits
```

---

### Key Files for Reference:

1. **Public API**: `/python/sglang/__init__.py` and `/python/sglang/lang/api.py`
2. **IR Definition**: `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py`
3. **Interpreter**: `/python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`
4. **Backend Interface**: `/python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`
5. **Grammar Management**: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`
6. **Grammar Backends**: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`, `xgrammar_backend.py`
7. **Tests**: `/python/sglang/test/test_programs.py`

167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total
244
